creator: chogata@moosefs.com
text:
Sorry for the delay.

We use protocol version 3.4 on systems that have FUSE 3. If not, we use 2.9. When we build packages, we always build with the latest possible library installed, but of course users can compile however they want. BUT we also say that some things simply don't work on FUSE 2, so we always recommend to install FUSE 3 whenever possible and to upgrade your OS to one that supports FUSE 3.

TL;DR: we use 3.4

Cache: when you mount MooseFS, you can choose cache mode. Default is AUTO, but the user can pick DIRECT, which will disable caches, writeback cache included. If it's enabled, the user can still set a direct flag for a selected file or files. Other than that, caches are on.
